patient_information.patient_address: 18 N Ontario St, New York, NY 21022

clinical_information.diagnosis: High-grade osteosarcoma of the distal femur (post-surgical, status post limb-sparing resection with prosthetic implant).
clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Neoadjuvant chemotherapy (March–April 2024): doxorubicin 75 mg/m2 IV q21 days, cisplatin 120 mg/m2 IV q21 days, high-dose methotrexate 12 g/m2 IV q14 days (3 cycles). Surgical resection (limb-sparing distal femur resection with prosthetic implant) on 05/01/2024. Adjuvant chemotherapy (May–Sept 2024): doxorubicin 37.5 mg/m2 IV q21 days, cisplatin 90 mg/m2 IV q21 days, methotrexate 12 g/m2 IV q14 days, ifosfamide 9 g/m2 IV q21 days with mesna (6 cycles completed through 09/04/2024). Recovery from surgery noted; follow-up imaging being reviewed (no imaging results documented in this note).
clinical_information.lab_summary: CBC demonstrates pancytopenia: leukopenia (WBC 2.5 x10^9/L), anemia (Hgb 9.5 g/dL, Hct 28%) and thrombocytopenia (Plt 75 x10^9/L). CMP shows otherwise preserved renal function and electrolytes but low albumin (3.0 g/dL) and hepatic abnormalities with mild–moderate transaminitis (AST 55, ALT 60 U/L), elevated alkaline phosphatase (150 U/L) and markedly increased LDH (450 U/L). Ferritin is substantially elevated at 800 ng/mL. Overall pattern is concerning for a systemic/inflammatory or hematologic process (eg, bone marrow disorder, hemophagocytic/inflammatory syndrome, or malignancy) with hepatic involvement; correlation with peripheral smear, reticulocyte count, inflammatory markers, viral hepatitis testing and consideration of bone marrow evaluation is indicated.
clinical_information.imaging_results: CT chest (09/04/24, noncontrast): 1.5 cm rounded, irregular, spiculated lesion in the left lower lobe suspicious for metastasis; several small pulmonary nodules up to 5 mm in both lungs; no mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy; no pleural effusion or pleural thickening. MRI left femur (09/04/24, T1/T2/STIR): new irregular distal femoral mass measuring ~4.0 cm with heterogeneous signal and T2 hyperintense areas suggesting edema/necrosis; tumor extends into surrounding soft tissues with irregular margins and adjacent muscle edema; extensive bone marrow edema around the lesion; thickening of surrounding soft tissues consistent with desmoplastic reaction; no new suspicious lesions in the proximal femur or surrounding structures. X-ray left knee (AP and lateral, 03/01/24): aggressive-appearing lytic lesion in the distal femur ~5 cm with cortical destruction and a soft tissue mass extending into the knee joint; periosteal reaction with sunburst pattern; no significant joint effusion; surrounding soft tissue thickening; no evidence of metastatic disease in surrounding bones or soft tissues. MRI findings listed for 03/02/24 contained no details.
clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Patient reports left thigh pain at the prior resection site and non-productive cough and fatigue for the last 2 weeks. Review of systems otherwise negative (no fever, no shortness of breath); specific severity or functional impact not quantified in the note.

1. Background:  
Afinitor (everolimus ) is a kin ase inhibitor  indicated for the treatment of postmenopausal women 
with advanced hormone receptor -positive, HER2- negative breast cancer in combination with 
Aromasin® (exemestane) after failure of treatment with Femara® (letrozole) or Arimidex® 
(anastrozole); in adults wi th progressive neuroendocrine tumors of pancreatic origin (PNET) and 
adults with progressive, well -differentiated, non- functional neuroendocrine tumors (NET) of 
gastrointestinal (GI) or lung origin that are unresectable, locally advanced or metastatic; adu lts 
with advanced renal cell carcinoma (RCC) after failure of treatment with Sutent® (sunitinib) or 
Nexavar® (sorafenib); adults with renal angiomyolipoma and t uberou

ii.  Patient is being treated with ovarian ablation/suppression  
 
  -OR- 
 
(c) Patient is male  
 
-AND-  
 
(6)  Used in combination with one of the following :

(c) Tamoxifen  
 
Authorization will be issued for 12 months.

decision.reason: The policy for Afinitor (everolimus) in osteosarcoma requires all listed criteria be met, including that everolimus be used in combination with Nexavar (sorafenib). The patient has a diagnosis of osteosarcoma with imaging suspicious for metastatic disease and has received prior systemic therapy (so would meet diagnosis, metastatic disease, and second-line requirements). However, the requested regimen is everolimus monotherapy (everolimus 5 mg/m2 once daily) with no documentation that it will be used in combination with Nexavar (sorafenib). Therefore the regimen does not meet the policy requirement that everolimus be used in combination with Nexavar (sorafenib).
